rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel State Compensation Insurance Fund'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Req. For Admissions, Set One; Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 10, 2012, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANTS VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. and VATCHE CABAYAN MEDICAL CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel State Compensation Insurance Fund's Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set 1 And Request For Admissions, Set 1; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Deft For Continuous Abuses Of The Disco Process And Ongoing Violations Of Court Orders And/Or For Order Compelling Further Responses To Requests For Production
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 10, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. and VATCHE CABAYAN MEDICAL CORPORATION'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant For Continuous Abuses Of The Discovery Process And Ongoing Violations Of Court Orders And/Or For Order Compelling Further Responses To Requests For Production. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. It would be helpful to the court if the parties were prepared to discuss their respective positions on the following issues: (1) whether the Court's prior order precluded redaction of documents and further withholding of documents on the basis of attorney-client privileged; and (2) whether bates stamped documents A329-9,409 and D1-2,220 should have been provided to the Court for in camera review pursuant to the Court's prior order. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DARYL BERKSON BRAND VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Release Of Deft Anthony Harge'S Metro Pcs Cell Phone Records And Motion To Compel Release Of William Brand'S Autopsy Photographs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 10, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFFS DARYL BRAND, THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM BRAND, DECEASED'S Motion To Compel Release Of Defendant Anthony Harge's Metro PCS Cell Phone Records And Motion To Compel Release Of William Brand'S Autopsy Photographs. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel is GRANTED with respect to both the METRO PCS records subpoenaed and the autopsy photographs. Defendant Anthony Harge shall, within three days of entry of this order, provide METRO PCS with whatever written consent METRO PCS requires in order to release the supoenaed records. The medical examiner shall provide counsel for Plaintiff, counsel for Defendant City and County of San Francisco, and counsel for Defendant Harge a complete copy of the autopsy photographs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN H. DANNER III et al VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion To 1) To Compel Responses-Documents Requested In Pltf'S Second Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents, Electronically Stored Information, And Tangible Things; 2) To Compel Verifications; 3) For An Order That The Genuinenes Of Documents And Truth Of Matters Specified Be Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2012, Line 4, pLAINTIFF JOHN DANNER's Motion To 1) To Compel Responses-Documents Requested In Pltf'S Second Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents, Electronically Stored Information, And Tangible Things; 2) To Compel Verifications; 3) For An Order That The Genuinenes Of Documents And Truth Of Matters Specified Be Admitted. The court adopts the recommendation and rationale of Pro Tem Judge Huibonhoa as follows: Denied. With respect to relevance to show what the missing H-20 documents would look like, there is insufficient evidence in this record that the H-40 documents are similar enough to the H-20 documents to be probative. Moreover, evidence submitted by the City makes clear that the same blank scoring key worksheets are provided to raters of the H-20 and the H-40 examination, and that those forms have been produced in this case. (See Declaration of Dave Johnson, at 10.) Plaintiffs' 1101(b) argument is speculative, itself based on the presumed similarity of the documents relating to the two examinations. Though mindful of the standards favoring discovery, the limited relevance of the H-40 documents needs to be weighed against their confidential nature, as set forth in the City's papers, and here, the confidentiality of the documents and their limited relevance weigh in favor of denying the motion. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN H. DANNER III et al VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel The Disclosure Of Information Wrongly Withheld In An "Opt-Out" Procedure Ordered By The Court From All Captioned Defts And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFFs JOHN DANNER, DONALD DURKEE, STEPHEN ENGLER, CARLOS HOY, ALFRED JOE, MARK KANE, ROHAN KNIGHT, ROBERTO LUCHA, ELI PAYTON, ROBERT MATEIK, JOHN SHANLEY, ERIC TANIMURA, PAUL URQUIAGA, VINCENT WONG, GIDEON PRICE, JOHN TUIASOSOPO, RAYCARDO AVILES' MOTION To Compel The Disclosure Of Information Wrongly Withheld In An "Opt-Out" Procedure Ordered By The Court From All Captioned Defendants And For Sanctions. The court adopts the recommendation and rationale of Pro Tem Judge Huibonhoa as follows: Granted. The "opt-out" procedure agreed to by the parties specifies a 30-day return, and the notice mailed to the candidates makes clear on its face that "the Department will not disclose your information if" certain conditions are met, including returning the notice within 30 days. Those candidates who failed to comply with these terms did not timely or effectively opt-out, and their information thus is subject to disclosure. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court adds that Defendant shall serve the disclosures no later than October 23, 2012. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RIMMA BOROKHOV et al VS. FREEDOM WEST HOMES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Deft To Provide Disco Responses; Req For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 10, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS RIMMA BOROKHOV, DAVID TSE and MICHAEL TO'S Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Discovery Responses; Request For Judicial Notice. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. It would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to present their positions on the following questions: (1) what evidence are Plaintiffs entitled to seek in furtherance of their theory of the case that Defendants engaged in alleged attempts to conceal their alleged bribery scheme; and (2) how the discovery at issue in plaintiffs' motions can be narrowed to provide plaintiffs with the information to which they are entitled while preventing undue burden on defendants. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: URI BEN HUR et al VS. YES TO, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANTs SCOTT POTTER, STEPHEN SIMON, LANCE KALISH, IDO LEFFLER, YES TO, INC., YES TO LTD.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted. Plaintiffs failed to provide any substantive opposition to the motion. Nor did plaintiffs provide a courtesy copy of their second statement of opposition as required by Local Rule 2.6B. The Court denies plaintiffs¿ request for a further continuance. The court is unaware of any facts that plaintiffs may seek to obtain that would enable them to defeat the motion or that any other useful purpose would be achieved by a further continuance. The prior continuance gave plaintiffs sufficient opportunity to submit substantive opposition. The court reluctantly concludes that, at this point, plaintiffs¿ sole or primary goal is to seek further delay. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: WELLS FARGO ADVISORS, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Special Mtn And Special Mtn To Strike Complaint And Req. For Attorney Fees And Pos

rulings.Case Title: SONYA ROSE VS. MERCY HOUSING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Deposition Of Pltf Sonya Rose; & Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 10, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT CATHOLIC CHARITIES CYO OF THE ARCHDIOCESE OF SAN FRANCISCO, MERCY HOUSING MANAGEMENT GROUP, INC.'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Sonya Rose; & Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel plaintiff Sonya Rose's attendance at a deposition is GRANTED. Ms. Rose shall appear for a deposition and produce the documents described in the deposition notice at the offices of Defendant's counsel within 30 days of the date of this order. The request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $720.00. Within 20 days of entry of this order, Plaintiff shall pay the sanctions via a check or money order payable to "Mercy Housing Management Group, Inc. or Catholic Charities CYO of the Archdiocese of San Francisco. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FIA CARD SERVICES, N.A. VS. MARIE R GILO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Of X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANT MARIE GILO's Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Of cross-Complaint. Granted. The dismissal of the Cross-Complaint violated Code of Civil Procedure sections 581(i) and (j) and is thus void on its face. Plaintiff's request to have the entire action reinstated is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff may file a motion seeking such relief. rulings.Case Title: RASHID ALATRAQCHI VS. METRO CAB LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Getting Like His Waybill He Employeeed By Defts Metro Cab, Llc And Richard Hybels, Owner
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2012, Line 17, PLAINTIFF RASHID ALATRAQCHI's MOTION To Getting Like His Waybill He Employeeed By Defendants Metro Cab, Llc And Richard Hybels, Owner. rulings.Case Title: BRIAN MOLONEY VS. RICHARD SCHUMACHER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2012, Line 21, DEFENDANT STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the contract and implied covenant claims and sustained without leave to amend as to the unjust enrichment claim. Plaintiff is granted leave to allege, if he can do so in good faith, the terms of the insurance policy that show that it was made for his benefit such that he can sue in a third party beneficiary capacity. Unjust enrichment is not a cognizable claim and, in all events, is inapplicable to the facts of this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. State Farm is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GUERNEVILLE BUSINESS CORPORATION, INC. A VS. ALBERT L BOASBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 10, 2012, Line 22, DEFENDANT ALBERT BOASBERG, ARTHUR FONDEN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the fourth, sixth, seventh, ninth and tenth causes of action, sustained without leave to amend as to the eleventh cause of action and overruled as to the twelfth cause of action. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. As to the fourth cause of action, Corporations Code section 1602 serves as a basis for directors of a corporation to sue to inspect corporate records. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to add a proper plaintiff who has standing to prosecute this claim. As to the sixth cause of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead that an actual agreement was entered into, the material terms of that agreement, and how Defendant acted to deprive Plaintiff the benefits of that agreement. As to the seventh, ninth, and tenth causes of action, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead facts showing either the discovery rule or doctrine of equitable tolling applies. In order for the discovery rule to apply, As to the tenth cause of action, Plaintiff improperly pleads two separate claims in one and negligent interference with contract is not a cause of action. (See Davis v. Nadrich (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1, 9.) As to the eleventh cause of action, there is no private right of action for embezzlement under Penal Code 503. As to the twelfth cause of action, Plaintiff has adequately pled a claim for conversion. All other grounds not specifically addressed lack merit. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Defendants are required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
